app.name: Arguslog
app.tagline: Less dashboard, more dialogue — error tracking, designed for agents.

admin.title: Platform admin
admin.subtitle: Logged in as platform administrator ({{email}}). All state-changing actions are written to the audit log.
admin.grantReasonHint: Visible to the customer in their billing page. Keep it short and explanatory ("Beta tester appreciation", "Refund credit for outage").

bonus.title: {{plan}} courtesy upgrade is active 🎁
bonus.body: Your plan is set to {{plan}} as a complimentary upgrade until {{until}} ({{untilRel}}). All paid features are unlocked. Caps and retention follow the bonus tier — billing will revert to the regular cadence afterwards.

orgSwitcher.label: Organization
orgSwitcher.deleteWarning: This is permanent. All projects, issues, events, alert rules, and destinations under this organization will be deleted.
orgSwitcher.deleteBody: Type the organization name to confirm.

auth.loginHint: Sign in with your organization account

onboarding.title: Create a project
onboarding.errorOrgName: Organization name must be at least 2 characters
onboarding.errorProjectName: Project name must be at least 2 characters
onboarding.successDsnHint: Copy this DSN into your app's config — it's the only way the SDK can authenticate to ingest.

projects.title: Projects
projects.empty: No projects yet — create one to start receiving events.
projects.orgNotFound: Organization not found in your memberships.
projects.archiveBody: Archive "{{name}}"? It will disappear from the project list, but its issues and events stay accessible by direct ID for incident review.
projects.archiveHint: This action is reversible from the database, but no UI to unarchive yet.
projects.dsnHint: Copy this DSN into your app's config — it's the only way the SDK can authenticate to ingest.
projects.gitHint: Link a public repo so the "Create release" form can fill Git ref + SHA from a branch dropdown. Public repos only.
projects.gitRepoHelpGithub: Example: acme/widgets — paste a full URL and we'll strip the host.
projects.gitRepoHelpGitlab: Example: group/project or group/sub/project — paste a full URL and we'll strip the host.

connect.title: Connect
connect.subtitle: Paste-ready config for every supported client. Copy the DSN into SDKs, the PAT into MCP servers and CLI — same project, two credentials, ten snippets.
connect.notFound: Project not found in this organization.

dsn.title: DSN
dsn.hint: Public credential safe to ship in client code. Authenticates ingest for THIS project only — cannot read issues, manage projects, or call admin APIs.
dsn.empty: This project has no active DSN yet — generate one to start receiving events.

pat.title: Personal access token
pat.hint: Private credential carrying your full membership scope. Required for MCP servers, the CLI, and any script that reads or writes through the API. Mint a fresh one below — the plaintext is shown exactly once.
pat.generate: Generate a PAT for this flow
pat.oneTimeWarning: Copy this now — it will never be shown again. Anyone with this token can act as you within the scopes you selected.

snippets.title: Paste-ready snippets
snippets.hint: Pick your stack and click Copy. Snippets fill in your DSN / PAT automatically; missing credentials show a placeholder so you can still copy the structure.

workflow.hint: Read · Eval · Triage · Loop — four canned playbooks your agent runs end-to-end. Discoverable via MCP prompts/list (Claude Code, Cursor, Continue) and paste-ready here for every other agent. Replace the <PLACEHOLDERS> with your project/issue/release values.

agent.hint: Copy one prompt, paste into your coding agent. It detects the stack, installs the matching Arguslog SDK, wires init(), and registers the MCP server — single paste, ~3 seconds of user effort.
agent.missingCredentials: Generate a DSN and PAT above; both get inlined into the prompts so the agent can finish the integration in one pass.
agent.provisioning: Setting up your one-paste install — minting DSN and PAT…
agent.rotateBody: You've been through Connect for this project before. Personal access tokens are shown in plaintext exactly once, so the prompts now carry a placeholder instead of a real PAT. Rotate to mint a new token and refill every snippet — the old one stays valid until you revoke it from Personal access tokens.

projectKeys.title: DSN keys
projectKeys.subtitle: Each key authenticates SDK traffic to ingest. Generate a new one to rotate; revoke any key you don't recognize.
projectKeys.useHint: Use DSN keys in your client SDK config (browser, mobile, server SDKs) to send events to ingest. They have one job — accepting event traffic for THIS project. They cannot read issues, manage projects, or call admin APIs. For those, generate a Personal Access Token from your account menu.
projectKeys.empty: No active keys yet — generate one to start receiving events.
projectKeys.revokeBody: After revoking, any SDK still configured with this DSN will start getting 401s. The action cannot be undone — generate a fresh key for the impacted service first if you need uninterrupted ingest.
projectKeys.rotateBody: Mints a new DSN and surfaces it once for copy. The old key stays active until you revoke it manually — gives running services time to pick up the new value before the old one stops working.
projectKeys.rotateHint: After copying the new DSN, revoke this old key from the row action when traffic has migrated.
projectKeys.revealedHint: This is the only time the full DSN will be shown. Copy it now — after closing this dialog the dashboard can only display its metadata, and no endpoint can ever return the secret again.

issues.title: Issues
issues.empty: No issues — your code is healthy.
issues.invalidProjectId: Invalid project — pick a project from the sidebar.

issueDetail.aiAnalysisEmpty: No AI analysis yet — runs automatically when a new-issue alert is configured with a hosted agent webhook.

slackIntegrations.title: Slack integration
slackIntegrations.lead: Install the Arguslog Slack app to run slash commands like /arguslog issues, /arguslog resolve <id>, and /arguslog release <version> from any channel.
slackIntegrations.empty: No Slack workspaces connected yet. Click Connect Slack to install the app.
slackIntegrations.disconnectBody: {{team}} will stop responding to /arguslog commands. You can reinstall any time.
slackIntegrations.createAlertDestinationTip: One-click: route Arguslog alert rules to {{channel}} in this workspace.
slackIntegrations.alertDestinationCreated: Created alert destination "{{name}}". Wire it to an alert rule from the Alerts page.
slackIntegrations.noWebhookHint: This workspace was installed without the incoming-webhook scope. Reinstall to enable one-click alerts.

alertDestinations.title: Alert destinations
alertDestinations.empty: No destinations yet — add one so alert rules have somewhere to fire.
alertDestinations.kindLocked: Kind cannot be changed after create — make a new destination instead.
alertDestinations.name: Name
alertDestinations.leaveBlankToKeep: Leave blank to keep the current value.
alertDestinations.secretEditHint: Leave blank to keep the current secret. Fill to rotate.
alertDestinations.telegramBotTokenHint: From @BotFather. Stored encrypted; never shown back.
alertDestinations.emailToHint: One or more email addresses. Type and press Enter, or paste comma-separated.
alertDestinations.slackWebhookUrlHint: Incoming webhook URL. The 🔔 button on the Slack integrations page creates one for you automatically.
alertDestinations.webhookUrlHint: Endpoint that will receive POST {alert} on rule fire.
alertDestinations.webhookSecretHint: If set, requests are signed and the receiver can verify origin.
alertDestinations.webhookSecretPlaceholder: Leave blank if your endpoint doesn't verify signatures
alertDestinations.githubOwnerHint: GitHub username or organisation that owns the repo (e.g. acme).
alertDestinations.githubRepoHint: Repository name only — no owner prefix.
alertDestinations.githubTokenHint: PAT scoped to this single repo with Contents: read, Issues: write, Pull requests: read. Copilot's coding agent acts as itself, not as this token — this token is only used by Arguslog to create the issue.
alertDestinations.githubAssigneeHint: GitHub username that auto-picks up assigned issues. Leave blank for the default: copilot-swe-agent.
alertDestinations.githubLabelsHint: Applied to every issue Arguslog creates. Leave blank for the default: arguslog-auto-triage.

alertRules.title: Alert rules
alertRules.empty: No alert rules yet — create one to start firing notifications on incoming issues.
alertRules.name: Name
alertRules.levelHint: Fire only for events at these severity levels. Leave empty to match all levels.
alertRules.windowHint: Only fire while the issue is fresher than this. Leave empty for no time limit.
alertRules.windowAdvancedHint: This rule uses an ISO-8601 duration that doesn't fit the simple unit form. Edit raw if needed.
alertRules.thresholdHint: Only fire after the issue has occurred at least this many times. Leave empty for 1.
alertRules.tagKeyHint: Scope to events carrying a specific SDK tag — e.g. env, region.
alertRules.tagValuesHint: Fire when the tag value is one of these. Type and press Enter, or paste comma-separated.
alertRules.throttleHint: Skip firing again within this window. 0 disables throttling.
alertRules.needDestinations: Add a destination first — rules need somewhere to fan out to.

releases.title: Releases
releases.intro: Tag deploy versions to attribute issues to a specific build. Upload source maps against a release for symbolicated stack traces.
releases.versionHint: Free-form string. Common patterns: semver (1.2.3), git SHA (a1b2c3d), or calver (2026.05.08).
releases.releasedAtHint: When the deploy actually went live. Independent of the row's createdAt.
releases.changelogHint: Markdown notes shown on the release detail page. 64 KiB max.
releases.deleteBody: Source-map artifacts uploaded against this release will be removed too. R2 blobs stay until the next maintenance sweep.
releases.empty: No releases yet — create one to start attributing issues to deploy versions.
releases.gitRefBranchesError: Couldn't load branches from {{provider}} — type the ref manually below.
releases.gitRefBranchesEmpty: No branches returned — type the ref manually below.
releases.gitRefHintWithRepo: Pick a branch on {{provider}} ({{repo}}) — Git SHA auto-fills.
releases.gitRefHintNoRepo: Link a Git repo in Project settings to enable the branch dropdown.

releaseDetail.issuesHint: Issues whose very first event tagged this release. The list is stable: subsequent events on the same issue don't move it to a later release. Capped at 200 rows.
releaseDetail.issuesEmpty: Clean ship — no new issues attributed to this release yet.
releaseDetail.sourceMapsHint: Upload .map files emitted by your build (Vite, Webpack, esbuild, …). The worker symbolicates events tagged with this release version by reading these files; without them, stack traces stay minified.
releaseDetail.empty:
No source maps uploaded yet. Drop a .map file with the button above, or run the equivalent from CI:
  arguslog sourcemaps upload dist/assets/bundle.js.map --project <id> --release <id> --path bundle.js
releaseDetail.deleteSourceMapConfirm: Delete the source map for {{path}}? This drops the row and the R2 blob — you'll need to re-upload to symbolicate this path again.

upload.title: Upload source map
upload.hint: The file is hashed locally, the metadata is registered with the API, then the bytes are uploaded directly to private storage. Your dashboard origin is never proxied for the blob itself.
upload.pathHint: What the SDK reports in stack frames — for a Vite/webpack bundle, this is the served JS path (e.g. "bundle.js" or "/assets/index.D7f2.js"). The worker matches frames against this string.

members.title: Members
members.intro: Owners can invite people to this organization and manage their roles. Invitees get an email with a link to sign in; if they don't have an account yet, the access binds automatically when they create one with this email.
members.inviteHint: They'll get an email pointing to the dashboard. Their access takes effect on first sign-in.
members.pendingTooltip: This invitee hasn't signed in yet. The row will update automatically on their first sign-in.
members.removeBody: They'll lose access to every project in this organization. PATs they own continue to work until revoked separately.
members.leaveBody: You'll lose access to every project here. You can be re-invited later by another owner.
members.empty: Just you so far. Invite teammates to share visibility into projects.

tokens.title: Personal access tokens
tokens.intro: Create a token to authenticate the CLI or scripts as you. Tokens carry your full membership scope, so treat them like passwords.
tokens.useHint: Use PATs for the MCP server, the CLI, and any server-side script that calls the Arguslog API on your behalf. Unlike a DSN key (which can only send events to a single project), a PAT can read issues, manage projects, and call admin endpoints across every org you belong to — subject to the scopes you select below.
tokens.issuedHint: Copy this now — it will not be shown again.
tokens.empty: No tokens yet — create one above.
tokens.revokeBody: This is irreversible. Any client using this token will start receiving 401 immediately.
tokens.scopesHint: Restrict what this token can do. Leaving every scope checked is the same as a fully-privileged token.
